# Vehicle Expense Tracking

> Record and categorise all costs attributable to individual fleet vehicles — fuel, maintenance, insurance, tolls, fines, and depreciation — and generate per-vehicle cost reports with budget variance.

**Category:** Workflow · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** fleet · vehicle · expenses · cost · reporting · budget · finance

## What this does

Record and categorise all costs attributable to individual fleet vehicles — fuel, maintenance, insurance, tolls, fines, and depreciation — and generate per-vehicle cost reports with budget variance.

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## Fields

- **vehicle** *(text, required)* — Vehicle
- **expense_date** *(date, required)* — Expense Date
- **expense_category** *(select, required)* — Expense Category
- **expense_description** *(text, required)* — Description
- **amount** *(number, required)* — Amount
- **currency** *(text, optional)* — Currency
- **payment_method** *(select, optional)* — Payment Method
- **vendor** *(text, optional)* — Vendor / Payee
- **reference_document** *(text, optional)* — Source Document Reference
- **source_type** *(select, optional)* — Source Type
- **receipt_attachment** *(file, optional)* — Receipt / Invoice
- **recorded_by** *(text, optional)* — Recorded By
- **budget_code** *(text, optional)* — Budget Code
- **approved_by** *(text, optional)* — Approved By

## What must be true

- **amount_positive:** Expense amount must be a positive value
- **date_not_future:** Expense date cannot be in the future
- **high_value_approval:** High-value expenses above a configurable threshold require finance manager approval before being posted
- **auto_generated_not_deletable:** Auto-generated expenses from fuel log, maintenance log, and depreciation cannot be manually deleted — they must be corrected via the originating record
- **cost_report_aggregation:** Per-vehicle cost reports aggregate all expense categories for a vehicle within a specified date range
- **budget_variance:** Budget variance is calculated when a monthly or annual budget is configured per vehicle or fleet category

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Budget Threshold Exceeded** — when monthly or annual budget is configured for the vehicle or fleet category; cumulative expenses in the current period exceed the budget threshold percentage, then Budget overrun alert is sent to stakeholders.
- **Cost Report Generated** — when vehicle filter and date range are provided, then Per-vehicle cost report is available showing spend by category with optional budget comparison.
- **Expense Approved** — when high-value expense is pending approval; finance manager reviews and approves, then Expense is posted and included in cost reports.
- **High Value Approval Required** — when amount exceeds the configured approval threshold; source_type is manual; approved_by is not set, then Expense is held pending finance manager approval.
- **Expense Recorded** — when vehicle exists in the fleet; expense_date is not in the future; Amount is positive; expense_category is valid, then Expense is attributed to the vehicle and available for cost reporting.

## Errors it can return

- `EXPENSE_INVALID_AMOUNT` — Expense amount must be greater than zero.
- `EXPENSE_FUTURE_DATE` — Expense date cannot be in the future.
- `EXPENSE_APPROVAL_REQUIRED` — This expense exceeds the approval limit and requires finance manager authorisation.

## Connects to

- **fuel-log** *(recommended)* — Fuel log totals are auto-posted as fuel expense records
- **vehicle-maintenance-log** *(recommended)* — Service costs from maintenance records are auto-posted as maintenance expense records
- **vehicle-insurance** *(recommended)* — Insurance premium amounts can be recorded as insurance expense records
- **vehicle-depreciation** *(recommended)* — Periodic depreciation amounts can be posted as depreciation expense records
- **vehicle-incident-log** *(recommended)* — Incident repair costs and insurance shortfalls roll into vehicle expense records
